title: Pediatric rash documentation

red_flags.text: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.
red_flags.rationale: Supports systemic, persistent, recurrent and uncertain-rash referral context for child rash documentation; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

assessment.text: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.
assessment.rationale: Supports fever and itch grouping without proving diagnosis for child rash documentation; no decision is generated.
assessment.evidence_extract: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

medication.text: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.
medication.rationale: Supports visual assessment, evolution, distribution, symptoms, recurrence, exposures and medicine history for child rash documentation; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Age, onset, evolution, distribution, morphology, itch, pain, fever, exposures, examination, diagnosis and clinician plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for child rash documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
